repo_summary.name: 6_mcp
repo_summary.overview: Week 6 MCP capstone project demonstrating a simulated autonomous trading floor built with OpenAI Agents SDK, multiple MCP servers, SQLite-backed state, tracing, and a Gradio dashboard.
repo_summary.final_goal: Teach how to move from MCP labs and notebooks into a reusable multi-agent system with modular orchestration, shared state, observability, and a runnable UI/runtime loop.
repo_summary.scope_note: This exploration was grounded directly in the five HTML study guides under tai_lieu plus the root-level .py and .ipynb files requested by the user.

modules_layers.name: Learning Layer
modules_layers.responsibility: Explain the course progression from MCP basics to the final autonomous traders capstone.

modules_layers.name: Domain State Layer
modules_layers.responsibility: Store account state, transaction history, logs, market cache, and seeded trader strategies.

modules_layers.name: MCP Surface Layer
modules_layers.responsibility: Expose and consume capabilities and resources through MCP stdio servers and clients.

modules_layers.name: Capability And Prompt Layer
modules_layers.responsibility: Configure tool surfaces, market data routing, and prompt templates.

modules_layers.name: Agent Orchestration Layer
modules_layers.responsibility: Build trader agents, wire researcher-as-tool, and schedule repeated autonomous runs.

modules_layers.name: Observability And UI Layer
modules_layers.responsibility: Capture traces into logs and display runtime state in a Gradio dashboard.

diagrams_source.runtime_data_flow:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Scheduler as trading_floor.py
  participant Trader as traders.py
  participant AccountsClient as accounts_client.py
  participant AccountsServer as accounts_server.py
  participant Tools as MCP tools
  participant DB as accounts.db
  participant UI as app.py

  Scheduler->>Trader: run()
  Trader->>AccountsClient: read account + strategy resources
  AccountsClient->>AccountsServer: MCP stdio request
  AccountsServer->>DB: read/write account state
  Trader->>Tools: research, market lookup, buy/sell, push
  Tools->>DB: write logs / state / market cache
  Trader->>DB: trace events via LogTracer
  UI->>DB: periodic refresh
  UI->>UI: render chart, holdings, transactions, logs
diagrams_source.setup_flow:
flowchart TD
  A["Install Python + uv"] --> B["Install Node/npx if using JS MCP servers"]
  B --> C["Set .env variables"]
  C --> D["Optional: uv run reset.py"]
  D --> E["uv run app.py"]
  D --> F["uv run trading_floor.py"]
  E --> G["Observe dashboard"]
  F --> G

open_questions: Where is the authoritative dependency manifest for exact package versions?
Is the root directory the canonical source, or is hieu/ intended as a maintained mirror or student workspace?
What is the expected behavior when required credentials for push notifications or market data are missing?
Should random price fallback in market.py be treated as a teaching convenience only, or is it expected in normal local runs?
What runtime path is recommended today for Windows users beyond the WSL note in the Day 1 materials?

notes: Architecture, workflow, and module responsibilities are strongly grounded in the read HTML study guides, notebooks, and root Python files.
Setup steps are inference-based because no commands were executed to install dependencies or run the project.
No automated tests or dependency manifest were found in the scoped read, which limits certainty around exact runtime expectations.
Some top-level folders outside the requested read scope were only inspected at presence level, not content level.
